title: Values, Expressions, Names, and Types · Code Recall
summary: Build a transparent unit and budget calculator whose intermediate values can be inspected and tested. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an inspectable unit and budget calculator after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish values, objects, names, assignment, equality, and identity in Python execution.
Use integers, floating-point values, decimal-sensitive decisions, arithmetic precedence, and division operators accurately.
Use Boolean values and None as distinct domain states instead of ambiguous numeric or text sentinels.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
